- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so m…
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep…
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/…
- claude-api: Build Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); or asks to add a Claude feature (prompt caching, ad…
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- cma-doctor: Validate Concept Model Kit installation
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- cma-normalize: Apply whitespace normalization to concept model documents
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-validate: Validate concept model links and annotations
- cma-render: Transform concept model to ClaudeMark format
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Focused build-out of a new binary and its tabtarget integration. Mechanical Rust + BUK wiring — the detection engine and parser already exist as library functions. No design ambiguity; the format decisions are made.
  
  ## Deliverables
  
  New binary `apcab` (App Batch): reads `*.html` files from an input directory, runs the parse and detection pipeline (apcrp + apcre), writes one `{stem}.assay.txt` per input file to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.
  
  Assay output format — guillemet-delimited elision markers with PHI flowing through:
  ```
  Patient: ‹RED:PATIENT Margaret Thornton›
  DOB: ‹RED:DOB 03/15/1952›
  ...presented to ‹YELLOW:FACILITY Maine Medical Center› with...
  ```
  Unelided text passes through verbatim. Unamended classification — no toggle logic, pure engine output.
  
  ## Implementation
  
  - `Tools/apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` — new binary entry point
    - Accept input directory as CLI argument
    - Glob `*.html`, parse each, run detection engine, render assay text
    - Write `{stem}.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`
    - No Tauri dependency — library crates only (apcrp, apcre, apcrm, apcrd)
  - Register `apcab` in `Cargo.toml` as a `[[bin]]` entry
  - Tabtarget `tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh` — folio via param1 (input directory path)
  - Enroll colophon `apcw-ba` in zipper, wire through workbench
  - Update `apck-claude-context.md` prefix tree and file mappings
  
  ## Default invocation
  
  ```
  tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh Tools/apck/test_fixtures/
  ```
  
  Emits `epic_progress_note.assay.txt` and `epic_geriatric_consult.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.
  
  ## Exit Criteria
  
  Both fixture HTML files produce assay output. Elision markers present for known PHI in each fixture. Output is readable as a document with classifications visible inline.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A9
2	
3	## Character
4	
5	Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.
6	
7	## Problem
8	
9	Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.
10	
11	## Solution
12	
13	Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
14	1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
15	2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
16	3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
17	4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
18	5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
19	6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
20	7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation
21	
22	## Key Design Decisions
23	
24	- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
25	- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
26	- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
27	- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
28	- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
29	- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
30	- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
31	
32	## Authoritative Documents
33	
34	| Document | Owns | Path |
35	|----------|------|------|
36	| Product Spec (APCS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCS-Specification.md` |
37	| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
38	| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
39	| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |

file.content:
# jjezs_paddock ₣A9

## Character

Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.

## Problem

Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.

## Solution

Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation

## Key Design Decisions

- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage

## Authoritative Documents

| Document | Owns | Path |
|----------|------|------|
| Product Spec (APCS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCS-Specification.md` |
| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |

# jjezs_pace ₢A9AAO

## Character

Focused build-out of a new binary and its tabtarget integration. Mechanical Rust + BUK wiring — the detection engine and parser already exist as library functions. No design ambiguity; the format decisions are made.

## Deliverables

New binary `apcab` (App Batch): reads `*.html` files from an input directory, runs the parse and detection pipeline (apcrp + apcre), writes one `{stem}.assay.txt` per input file to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.

Assay output format — guillemet-delimited elision markers with PHI flowing through:
```
Patient: ‹RED:PATIENT Margaret Thornton›
DOB: ‹RED:DOB 03/15/1952›
...presented to ‹YELLOW:FACILITY Maine Medical Center› with...
```
Unelided text passes through verbatim. Unamended classification — no toggle logic, pure engine output.

## Implementation

- `Tools/apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` — new binary entry point
  - Accept input directory as CLI argument
  - Glob `*.html`, parse each, run detection engine, render assay text
  - Write `{stem}.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`
  - No Tauri dependency — library crates only (apcrp, apcre, apcrm, apcrd)
- Register `apcab` in `Cargo.toml` as a `[[bin]]` entry
- Tabtarget `tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh` — folio via param1 (input directory path)
- Enroll colophon `apcw-ba` in zipper, wire through workbench
- Update `apck-claude-context.md` prefix tree and file mappings

## Default invocation

```
tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh Tools/apck/test_fixtures/
```

Emits `epic_progress_note.assay.txt` and `epic_geriatric_consult.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.

## Exit Criteria

Both fixture HTML files produce assay output. Elision markers present for known PHI in each fixture. Output is readable as a document with classifications visible inline.

**Heat ₣A9: apck-1-proof-of-life** (racing, 10/15 done)

**Paddock**: Ann's PHI Clipbuddy — Tauri desktop app that intercepts Epic EHR clipboard, detects PHI via three-tier engine, presents triage view, writes anonymized text. Specs, scaffold, clipboard parsing, detection engine, frontend, deploy, and dictionary work are all done.

**Recent work**: Paces A-K complete — fixture walkthrough debug was the last wrapped pace.

**Next pace: ₢A9AAO — batch-assay-mode** (rough)

Build a new `apcab` binary that batch-processes `*.html` fixture files through the existing parse+detect pipeline and writes `{stem}.assay.txt` with guillemet-delimited elision markers showing PHI classifications inline. Also: tabtarget `apcw-ba`, zipper enrollment, workbench wiring, context doc update.

**Approach:**
1. Create `apcab_main.rs` — CLI binary reading input dir, globbing `*.html`, running `apcrp` parse + `apcre` detect, rendering assay text with `‹SEVERITY:CATEGORY text›` markers, writing to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`
2. Register `[[bin]]` in `Cargo.toml`, create tabtarget, enroll in zipper + workbench
3. Update `apck-claude-context.md` prefix tree
4. Run against test fixtures and verify output

**Execution**: Sequential sonnet would suffice (mechanical wiring), but since we're already on opus, straightforward sequential here — single binary + a few registration files.

19	//! Fixture loader — writes synthetic Epic clipboard HTML for testing.
20	//! Usage: cargo run --bin apcal [fixture-file]
21	
22	// RCG output discipline: all emission via apcrl_*! — no direct println!/eprintln!
23	
24	fn main() {
25	    let args: Vec<String> = std::env::args().collect();
26	    if args.len() != 2 {
27	        apcd::apcrl_fatal_now!("usage: apcal <fixture-file-path>");
28	    }
29	    let path = &args[1];
30	
31	    let html = std::fs::read_to_string(path)
32	        .unwrap_or_else(|e| apcd::apcrl_fatal_now!("failed to read {}: {}", path, e));
33	
34	    let mut clipboard = arboard::Clipboard::new()
35	        .unwrap_or_else(|e| apcd::apcrl_fatal_now!("failed to open clipboard: {}", e));
36	
37	    clipboard.set_html(&html, Some(&html))
38	        .unwrap_or_else(|e| apcd::apcrl_fatal_now!("failed to set clipboard HTML: {}", e));
39	
40	    apcd::apcrl_info_now!("loaded {} onto clipboard as HTML ({} bytes)", path, html.len());
41	}
42

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# APCW Workbench - Routes APCK commands to CLIs via zipper registry
#
# All commands dispatch via buz_exec_lookup (see apcz_zipper.sh for colophon mapping).

set -euo pipefail

# Get script directory
APCW_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

# Source dependencies
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
source "${APCW_SCRIPT_DIR}/apcz_zipper.sh"

# Show filename on each displayed line
buc_context "${0##*/}"

# Kindle dispatch and zipper registry
zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle
zbuz_kindle
zapcz_kindle

# Verbose output if BURE_VERBOSE is set
apcw_show() {
  test "${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}" != "1" || echo "APCWSHOW: $*"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

apcw_route() {
  local z_command="$1"
  shift

  apcw_show "Routing command: ${z_command} with args: $*"

  zburd_sentinel
  zapcz_healthcheck

  apcw_show "BURD environment verified"

  buz_exec_lookup "${z_command}" "${APCW_SCRIPT_DIR}" "$@"
}

apcw_main() {
  local z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"

  apcw_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

apcw_main "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# APCZ Zipper - Colophon registry for APCK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZAPCZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZAPCZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zapcz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZAPCZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "apcz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled
  zbuz_sentinel

  # Build — Tauri build and development (apcw-)
  buz_group APCZ__GROUP_BUILD   "apcw-"   "Build — Tauri build and development"
  buz_enroll APCZ_BUILD          "apcw-b"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_build"         ""  "cargo tauri build (release)"
  buz_enroll APCZ_RUN            "apcw-r"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_run"           ""  "cargo tauri dev (local development)"
  buz_enroll APCZ_DEPLOY         "apcw-d"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_deploy"        ""  "Build + scp to anns-macbook-air"
  buz_enroll APCZ_FIXTURE_LOAD   "apcw-fl" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_fixture_load"  "imprint"  "Run apcal to load fixture HTML onto clipboard"
  buz_enroll APCZ_TEST           "apcw-t"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_test"          ""  "cargo test in apcd/"
  buz_enroll APCZ_DICT_REFRESH  "apcw-dr" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_dictionary_refresh" "" "Refresh dictionaries from public sources"

  readonly ZAPCZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zapcz_healthcheck() {
  zapcz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zapcz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZAPCZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module apcz not kindled - call zapcz_kindle first"
}

# eof

20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
24	
25	ZAPCC_APCD_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/apcd"
26	ZAPCC_MANIFEST="${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}/Cargo.toml"
27	
28	######################################################################
29	# Commands
30	
31	apcc_build() {
32	  buc_step "Building Tauri app (release)"
33	  (
34	    cd "${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to cd to ${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}"
35	    cargo tauri build     || buc_die "cargo tauri build failed"
36	  ) || buc_die "Build subshell failed"
37	}
38	
39	apcc_run() {
40	  buc_step "Running Tauri app (development)"
41	  cargo run --bin apcap --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
42	    || buc_die "cargo run failed"
43	}
44	
45	apcc_deploy() {
46	  buc_step "Building for deployment"
47	  (
48	    cd "${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to cd to ${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}"
49	    cargo tauri build     || buc_die "cargo tauri build failed"
50	  ) || buc_die "Build subshell failed"
51	
52	  local -r z_bundle_dir="${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}/target/release/bundle/macos"
53	  local -r z_size_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_size.txt"
54	  local -r z_size_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_size_stderr.txt"
55	  local -r z_ssh_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_ssh_stderr.txt"
56	  local -r z_scp_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_scp_stderr.txt"
57	
58	  # Find first .app bundle via glob
59	  local z_app_name=""
60	  for z_app_name in "${z_bundle_dir}"/*.app; do
61	    test -d "${z_app_name}" && break
62	    z_app_name=""
63	  done
64	  test -n "${z_app_name}" || buc_die "No .app bundle found in ${z_bundle_dir}"
65	
66	  # Measure bundle size via temp file
67	  du -sh "${z_app_name}" > "${z_size_file}" 2>"${z_size_stderr}" \
68	    || buc_die "Failed to measure bundle — see ${z_size_stderr}"
69	  local -r z_size_raw=$(<"${z_size_file}")
70	  test -n "${z_size_raw}" || buc_die "Empty size output from ${z_size_file}"
71	  local -r z_bundle_size="${z_size_raw%%$'\t'*}"
72	
73	  buc_step "Deploying to anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
74	  ssh anns-macbook-air 'rm -rf /Users/Shared/apcua/*.app' 2>"${z_ssh_stderr}" \
75	    || buc_die "Failed to clean staging dir — see ${z_ssh_stderr}"
76	  scp -r "${z_app_name}" anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/ 2>"${z_scp_stderr}" \
77	    || buc_die "Failed to deploy bundle — see ${z_scp_stderr}"
78	  buc_step "Deploy complete: ${z_app_name##*/} (${z_bundle_size}) → anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
79	}
80	
81	apcc_fixture_load() {
82	  local z_folio="${BUZ_FOLIO:?fixture name required}"
83	  local z_fixture_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/test_fixtures"
84	  local z_fixture_file
85	  case "${z_folio}" in
86	    progress)  z_fixture_file="${z_fixture_dir}/epic_progress_note.html" ;;
87	    geriatric) z_fixture_file="${z_fixture_dir}/epic_geriatric_consult.html" ;;
88	    *) buc_die "Unknown fixture: ${z_folio}" ;;
89	  esac
90	  test -f "${z_fixture_file}" || buc_die "Fixture file not found: ${z_fixture_file}"
91	  buc_step "Loading fixture '${z_folio}' onto clipboard"
92	  cargo run --bin apcal --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" -- "${z_fixture_file}" \
93	    || buc_die "cargo run apcal failed"
94	}
95	
96	apcc_test() {
97	  buc_step "Running cargo test"
98	  local -r z_output_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_test_output.txt"
99	  cargo test --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
100	    > "${z_output_file}" 2>&1 \
101	    || { cat "${z_output_file}"; buc_die "cargo test failed"; }
102	  cat "${z_output_file}"
103	  local -r z_total_passed=$(grep -c '^test .* ok$' "${z_output_file}" || true)
104	  buc_step "Tests complete: ${z_total_passed} passed"
105	}
106	
107	apcc_dictionary_refresh() {
108	  buc_step "Refreshing dictionaries from public sources"
109	  cargo run --bin apcad --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
110	    || buc_die "cargo run apcad failed"
111	}
112	
113	######################################################################
114	# Furnish and dispatch
115	
116	zapcc_furnish() {
117	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR" "Temporary directory (dispatch-provided)"
118	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
119	}
120	
121	buc_execute apcc_ "APCK CLI" zapcc_furnish "$@"
122	
123	# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# APCC CLI - Command implementations for APCK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

ZAPCC_APCD_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/apcd"
ZAPCC_MANIFEST="${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}/Cargo.toml"

######################################################################
# Commands

apcc_build() {
  buc_step "Building Tauri app (release)"
  (
    cd "${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to cd to ${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}"
    cargo tauri build     || buc_die "cargo tauri build failed"
  ) || buc_die "Build subshell failed"
}

apcc_run() {
  buc_step "Running Tauri app (development)"
  cargo run --bin apcap --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
    || buc_die "cargo run failed"
}

apcc_deploy() {
  buc_step "Building for deployment"
  (
    cd "${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to cd to ${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}"
    cargo tauri build     || buc_die "cargo tauri build failed"
  ) || buc_die "Build subshell failed"

  local -r z_bundle_dir="${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}/target/release/bundle/macos"
  local -r z_size_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_size.txt"
  local -r z_size_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_size_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_ssh_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_ssh_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_scp_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_scp_stderr.txt"

  # Find first .app bundle via glob
  local z_app_name=""
  for z_app_name in "${z_bundle_dir}"/*.app; do
    test -d "${z_app_name}" && break
    z_app_name=""
  done
  test -n "${z_app_name}" || buc_die "No .app bundle found in ${z_bundle_dir}"

  # Measure bundle size via temp file
  du -sh "${z_app_name}" > "${z_size_file}" 2>"${z_size_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to measure bundle — see ${z_size_stderr}"
  local -r z_size_raw=$(<"${z_size_file}")
  test -n "${z_size_raw}" || buc_die "Empty size output from ${z_size_file}"
  local -r z_bundle_size="${z_size_raw%%$'\t'*}"

  buc_step "Deploying to anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
  ssh anns-macbook-air 'rm -rf /Users/Shared/apcua/*.app' 2>"${z_ssh_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to clean staging dir — see ${z_ssh_stderr}"
  scp -r "${z_app_name}" anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/ 2>"${z_scp_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to deploy bundle — see ${z_scp_stderr}"
  buc_step "Deploy complete: ${z_app_name##*/} (${z_bundle_size}) → anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
}

apcc_fixture_load() {
  local z_folio="${BUZ_FOLIO:?fixture name required}"
  local z_fixture_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/test_fixtures"
  local z_fixture_file
  case "${z_folio}" in
    progress)  z_fixture_file="${z_fixture_dir}/epic_progress_note.html" ;;
    geriatric) z_fixture_file="${z_fixture_dir}/epic_geriatric_consult.html" ;;
    *) buc_die "Unknown fixture: ${z_folio}" ;;
  esac
  test -f "${z_fixture_file}" || buc_die "Fixture file not found: ${z_fixture_file}"
  buc_step "Loading fixture '${z_folio}' onto clipboard"
  cargo run --bin apcal --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" -- "${z_fixture_file}" \
    || buc_die "cargo run apcal failed"
}

apcc_test() {
  buc_step "Running cargo test"
  local -r z_output_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_test_output.txt"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
    > "${z_output_file}" 2>&1 \
    || { cat "${z_output_file}"; buc_die "cargo test failed"; }
  cat "${z_output_file}"
  local -r z_total_passed=$(grep -c '^test .* ok$' "${z_output_file}" || true)
  buc_step "Tests complete: ${z_total_passed} passed"
}

apcc_dictionary_refresh() {
  buc_step "Refreshing dictionaries from public sources"
  cargo run --bin apcad --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
    || buc_die "cargo run apcad failed"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and dispatch

zapcc_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR" "Temporary directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
}

buc_execute apcc_ "APCK CLI" zapcc_furnish "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# APCC CLI - Command implementations for APCK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

ZAPCC_APCD_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/apcd"
ZAPCC_MANIFEST="${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}/Cargo.toml"

######################################################################
# Commands

apcc_build() {
  buc_step "Building Tauri app (release)"
  (
    cd "${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to cd to ${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}"
    cargo tauri build     || buc_die "cargo tauri build failed"
  ) || buc_die "Build subshell failed"
}

apcc_run() {
  buc_step "Running Tauri app (development)"
  cargo run --bin apcap --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
    || buc_die "cargo run failed"
}

apcc_deploy() {
  buc_step "Building for deployment"
  (
    cd "${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to cd to ${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}"
    cargo tauri build     || buc_die "cargo tauri build failed"
  ) || buc_die "Build subshell failed"

  local -r z_bundle_dir="${ZAPCC_APCD_DIR}/target/release/bundle/macos"
  local -r z_size_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_size.txt"
  local -r z_size_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_size_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_ssh_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_ssh_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_scp_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_deploy_scp_stderr.txt"

  # Find first .app bundle via glob
  local z_app_name=""
  for z_app_name in "${z_bundle_dir}"/*.app; do
    test -d "${z_app_name}" && break
    z_app_name=""
  done
  test -n "${z_app_name}" || buc_die "No .app bundle found in ${z_bundle_dir}"

  # Measure bundle size via temp file
  du -sh "${z_app_name}" > "${z_size_file}" 2>"${z_size_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to measure bundle — see ${z_size_stderr}"
  local -r z_size_raw=$(<"${z_size_file}")
  test -n "${z_size_raw}" || buc_die "Empty size output from ${z_size_file}"
  local -r z_bundle_size="${z_size_raw%%$'\t'*}"

  buc_step "Deploying to anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
  ssh anns-macbook-air 'rm -rf /Users/Shared/apcua/*.app' 2>"${z_ssh_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to clean staging dir — see ${z_ssh_stderr}"
  scp -r "${z_app_name}" anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/ 2>"${z_scp_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to deploy bundle — see ${z_scp_stderr}"
  buc_step "Deploy complete: ${z_app_name##*/} (${z_bundle_size}) → anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/"
}

apcc_fixture_load() {
  local z_folio="${BUZ_FOLIO:?fixture name required}"
  local z_fixture_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/test_fixtures"
  local z_fixture_file
  case "${z_folio}" in
    progress)  z_fixture_file="${z_fixture_dir}/epic_progress_note.html" ;;
    geriatric) z_fixture_file="${z_fixture_dir}/epic_geriatric_consult.html" ;;
    *) buc_die "Unknown fixture: ${z_folio}" ;;
  esac
  test -f "${z_fixture_file}" || buc_die "Fixture file not found: ${z_fixture_file}"
  buc_step "Loading fixture '${z_folio}' onto clipboard"
  cargo run --bin apcal --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" -- "${z_fixture_file}" \
    || buc_die "cargo run apcal failed"
}

apcc_test() {
  buc_step "Running cargo test"
  local -r z_output_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/apcc_test_output.txt"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
    > "${z_output_file}" 2>&1 \
    || { cat "${z_output_file}"; buc_die "cargo test failed"; }
  cat "${z_output_file}"
  local -r z_total_passed=$(grep -c '^test .* ok$' "${z_output_file}" || true)
  buc_step "Tests complete: ${z_total_passed} passed"
}

apcc_dictionary_refresh() {
  buc_step "Refreshing dictionaries from public sources"
  cargo run --bin apcad --manifest-path "${ZAPCC_MANIFEST}" \
    || buc_die "cargo run apcad failed"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and dispatch

zapcc_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR" "Temporary directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
}

buc_execute apcc_ "APCK CLI" zapcc_furnish "$@"

# eof

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# APCZ Zipper - Colophon registry for APCK workbench dispatch

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard
test -z "${ZAPCZ_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZAPCZ_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Colophon registry initialization

zapcz_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZAPCZ_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "apcz already kindled"

  # Verify buz zipper is kindled
  zbuz_sentinel

  # Build — Tauri build and development (apcw-)
  buz_group APCZ__GROUP_BUILD   "apcw-"   "Build — Tauri build and development"
  buz_enroll APCZ_BUILD          "apcw-b"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_build"         ""  "cargo tauri build (release)"
  buz_enroll APCZ_RUN            "apcw-r"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_run"           ""  "cargo tauri dev (local development)"
  buz_enroll APCZ_DEPLOY         "apcw-d"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_deploy"        ""  "Build + scp to anns-macbook-air"
  buz_enroll APCZ_FIXTURE_LOAD   "apcw-fl" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_fixture_load"  "imprint"  "Run apcal to load fixture HTML onto clipboard"
  buz_enroll APCZ_TEST           "apcw-t"  "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_test"          ""  "cargo test in apcd/"
  buz_enroll APCZ_DICT_REFRESH  "apcw-dr" "apcc_cli.sh" "apcc_dictionary_refresh" "" "Refresh dictionaries from public sources"

  readonly ZAPCZ_KINDLED=1
}

######################################################################
# Healthcheck (validates all enrolled tabtargets exist on disk)

zapcz_healthcheck() {
  zapcz_sentinel
  buz_healthcheck
}

######################################################################
# Internal sentinel

zapcz_sentinel() {
  test "${ZAPCZ_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module apcz not kindled - call zapcz_kindle first"
}

# eof

content.content:
1	## Ann's PHI Clipbuddy Kit (APCK)
2	
3	APCK is a Tauri desktop app that intercepts clipboard content from Epic EHR, detects PHI via a three-tier engine (regex, label-anchored, dictionary), presents a red/yellow/grey triage view, and writes anonymized plain text to clipboard for pasting into Open Evidence.
4	
5	### File Acronym Mappings
6	
7	| Acronym | File | Description |
8	|---------|------|-------------|
9	| **APCS** | `apck/APCS-Specification.md` | Product spec — UX, workflow, deployment |
10	| **APCPS** | `apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` | Prototype spec — detection pipeline, tech stack |
11	| **APCW** | `apck/apcw_workbench.sh` | Workbench |
12	| **APCZ** | `apck/apcz_zipper.sh` | Zipper enrollment |
13	| **APCC** | `apck/apcc_cli.sh` | CLI command implementations |
14	| **APCAP** | `apck/apcd/src/apcap_main.rs` | Tauri app entry point |
15	| **APCAL** | `apck/apcd/src/apcal_main.rs` | Fixture loader (clipboard writer) |
16	| **APCAD** | `apck/apcd/src/apcad_main.rs` | Dictionary refresh (downloads public sources, regenerates dictionaries) |
17	
18	**Source modules:**
19	
20	| Source | Prefix | Test | Prefix | Purpose |
21	|--------|--------|------|--------|---------|
22	| `apcrl_log.rs` | `apcrl` | — | — | Logging macros (`apcrl_info!`, `apcrl_error!`, `apcrl_fatal!`) |
23	| `apcre_engine.rs` | `apcre` | `apcte_engine.rs` | `apcte` | PHI detection orchestrator |
24	| `apcrp_parse.rs` | `apcrp` | `apctp_parse.rs` | `apctp` | HTML clipboard parsing |
25	| `apcrm_match.rs` | `apcrm` | `apctm_match.rs` | `apctm` | Dictionary/regex matching |
26	| `apcrd_dictionaries.rs` | `apcrd` | `apctd_dictionaries.rs` | `apctd` | Dictionary loading |
27	| `apcru_update.rs` | `apcru` | — | — | Self-update watcher (no unit tests — I/O + process) |
28	
29	**Other key paths:**
30	- `Tools/apck/apcd/ui/` — Frontend (HTML/CSS/JS)
31	- `Tools/apck/apcd/dictionaries/` — Blacklist/whitelist data files
32	- `Tools/apck/test_fixtures/` — Synthetic Epic clipboard data
33	
34	**Rust conventions:** Follow RCG (`Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md`). Source prefix: `apcr{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Test prefix: `apct{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Classifier matches between source and test.
35	
36	### Tabtargets
37	
38	| Tabtarget | Colophon | Purpose |
39	|-----------|----------|---------|
40	| `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` | `apcw-b` | `cargo tauri build` (release) |
41	| `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` | `apcw-r` | `cargo run --bin apcap` (local development) |

## Ann's PHI Clipbuddy Kit (APCK)

APCK is a Tauri desktop app that intercepts clipboard content from Epic EHR, detects PHI via a three-tier engine (regex, label-anchored, dictionary), presents a red/yellow/grey triage view, and writes anonymized plain text to clipboard for pasting into Open Evidence.

### File Acronym Mappings

| Acronym | File | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| **APCS** | `apck/APCS-Specification.md` | Product spec — UX, workflow, deployment |
| **APCPS** | `apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` | Prototype spec — detection pipeline, tech stack |
| **APCW** | `apck/apcw_workbench.sh` | Workbench |
| **APCZ** | `apck/apcz_zipper.sh` | Zipper enrollment |
| **APCC** | `apck/apcc_cli.sh` | CLI command implementations |
| **APCAP** | `apck/apcd/src/apcap_main.rs` | Tauri app entry point |
| **APCAL** | `apck/apcd/src/apcal_main.rs` | Fixture loader (clipboard writer) |
| **APCAD** | `apck/apcd/src/apcad_main.rs` | Dictionary refresh (downloads public sources, regenerates dictionaries) |

**Source modules:**

| Source | Prefix | Test | Prefix | Purpose |
|--------|--------|------|--------|---------|
| `apcrl_log.rs` | `apcrl` | — | — | Logging macros (`apcrl_info!`, `apcrl_error!`, `apcrl_fatal!`) |
| `apcre_engine.rs` | `apcre` | `apcte_engine.rs` | `apcte` | PHI detection orchestrator |
| `apcrp_parse.rs` | `apcrp` | `apctp_parse.rs` | `apctp` | HTML clipboard parsing |
| `apcrm_match.rs` | `apcrm` | `apctm_match.rs` | `apctm` | Dictionary/regex matching |
| `apcrd_dictionaries.rs` | `apcrd` | `apctd_dictionaries.rs` | `apctd` | Dictionary loading |
| `apcru_update.rs` | `apcru` | — | — | Self-update watcher (no unit tests — I/O + process) |

**Other key paths:**
- `Tools/apck/apcd/ui/` — Frontend (HTML/CSS/JS)
- `Tools/apck/apcd/dictionaries/` — Blacklist/whitelist data files
- `Tools/apck/test_fixtures/` — Synthetic Epic clipboard data

**Rust conventions:** Follow RCG (`Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md`). Source prefix: `apcr{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Test prefix: `apct{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Classifier matches between source and test.

### Tabtargets

| Tabtarget | Colophon | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` | `apcw-b` | `cargo tauri build` (release) |
| `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` | `apcw-r` | `cargo run --bin apcap` (local development) |
| `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` | `apcw-d` | Build + scp to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/` |
| `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` | `apcw-fl` | Run `apcal` to load fixture HTML onto clipboard |
| `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` | `apcw-t` | `cargo test` in `apcd/` |
| `tt/apcw-dr.DictionaryRefresh.sh` | `apcw-dr` | `cargo run --bin apcad` (refresh dictionaries from public sources) |

### Prefix Tree

```
apc  (non-terminal)
├── apca   (non-terminal)
│   ├── apcad  — App Dictionary binary (refresh from public sources)
│   ├── apcal  — App Loader binary (fixture clipboard tool)
│   └── apcap  — App Prototype binary (Tauri main)
├── apcc   — CLI command implementations
├── apcd   — Rust/Tauri source directory
│   └── apcrl  — Logging macros (info, error, fatal with file/line)
├── apck   — kit directory
├── apcps  — prototype specification document
├── apcs   — product specification document
├── apcu   (non-terminal)
│   └── apcua — update staging directory (/Users/Shared/apcua/)
├── apcw   — workbench
└── apcz   — zipper
```

### Detection Architecture

Three tiers, merged by highest severity:
- **Tier 1 — Regex (RED):** SSN, phone, email, dates, addresses, zip, labeled identifiers
- **Tier 2 — Label-anchored (RED):** Words following Epic labels (Patient:, Attending:, Facility:, etc.)
- **Tier 3 — Dictionary (YELLOW/PASS):** aho-corasick blacklist scan, whitelist filter, collision → YELLOW

### Deploy Workflow

1. `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` — produces `.app` bundle
2. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` — scp to Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`
3. App self-updates: watches staging directory, copies new bundle over self, relaunches

input.old_string: | **APCAD** | `apck/apcd/src/apcad_main.rs` | Dictionary refresh (downloads public sources, regenerates dictionaries) |
input.new_string:
| **APCAD** | `apck/apcd/src/apcad_main.rs` | Dictionary refresh (downloads public sources, regenerates dictionaries) |
| **APCAB** | `apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` | Batch assay (run detection pipeline on HTML directory, write assay output) |

1. `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` — produces `.app` bundle
2. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` — scp to Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`
3. App self-updates: watches staging directory, copies new bundle over self, relaunches

lines: +│   ├── apcab  — App Batch binary (assay — detection pipeline on HTML files)
│   ├── apcad  — App Dictionary binary (refresh from public sources)
│   ├── apcal  — App Loader binary (fixture clipboard tool)

## Paces

### batch-assay-mode (₢A9AAO) [rough]

## Character

Focused build-out of a new binary and its tabtarget integration. Mechanical Rust + BUK wiring — the detection engine and parser already exist as library functions. No design ambiguity; the format decisions are made.

## Deliverables

New binary `apcab` (App Batch): reads `*.html` files from an input directory, runs the parse and detection pipeline (apcrp + apcre), writes one `{stem}.assay.txt` per input file to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.

Assay output format — guillemet-delimited elision markers with PHI flowing through:
```
Patient: ‹RED:PATIENT Margaret Thornton›
DOB: ‹RED:DOB 03/15/1952›
...presented to ‹YELLOW:FACILITY Maine Medical Center› with...
```
Unelided text passes through verbatim. Unamended classification — no toggle logic, pure engine output.

## Implementation

- `Tools/apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` — new binary entry point
  - Accept input directory as CLI argument
  - Glob `*.html`, parse each, run detection engine, render assay text
  - Write `{stem}.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`
  - No Tauri dependency — library crates only (apcrp, apcre, apcrm, apcrd)
- Register `apcab` in `Cargo.toml` as a `[[bin]]` entry
- Tabtarget `tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh` — folio via param1 (input directory path)
- Enroll colophon `apcw-ba` in zipper, wire through workbench
- Update `apck-claude-context.md` prefix tree and file mappings

## Default invocation

```
tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh Tools/apck/test_fixtures/
```

Emits `epic_progress_note.assay.txt` and `epic_geriatric_consult.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.

## Exit Criteria

Both fixture HTML files produce assay output. Elision markers present for known PHI in each fixture. Output is readable as a document with classifications visible inline.

### fixture-walkthrough-debug (₢A9AAK) [rough]

## Character

Guided manual verification. No code changes expected — this is a structured test session using the two synthetic fixtures to confirm the full pipeline works end-to-end on the developer's machine. Debugging character: if something fails, diagnose and fix inline.

## Prerequisites

- App builds clean: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` or `cargo build` in apcd/
- Two fixture loaders available: `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` and `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh`

## Walkthrough — Progress Note Fixture

1. Run `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` to launch the app
2. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` to load progress note HTML onto clipboard
3. Switch to the app window — triage view should appear
4. Confirm: RED findings include Patient (Margaret Thornton), Attending (Dr. Sarah Whitfield), DOB date, SSN, MRN, phone, email, address, URL
5. Confirm: YELLOW findings include dictionary-matched names in narrative
6. Confirm: DOB shows the date highlighted, not an age yet (age appears in anonymized output)
7. Toggle two YELLOW items to PASS — confirm preview updates (grey styling)
8. Toggle one RED item to PASS — confirm preview updates
9. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" — button should show "Copied!"
10. Paste into a text editor. Verify:
    - Elided items show category placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[SSN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[ADDRESS]`, etc.
    - DOB shows `Age: 74` (or appropriate computed age)
    - Items toggled to PASS show original text
    - Line breaks and section structure preserved — readable, not a wall
    - No PHI leaks in elided regions

## Walkthrough — Geriatric Consult Fixture

1. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh` to load geriatric HTML onto clipboard
2. Switch to app — triage view should refresh with new content
3. Confirm: clinical content detected, findings populated
4. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" with all defaults (everything elided)
5. Paste and verify: all PHI replaced, structure preserved
6. If geriatric fixture has a DOB for patient >89 years old, confirm `Age: 90+` appears

## Edge Cases to Probe

- Refocus without changing clipboard — should show "unchanged", triage preserved
- Copy non-clinical text (e.g., "hello world") — should show non-clinical diagnostic, triage cleared
- After non-clinical, click "Copy Anonymized" — should show error (no clinical data)
- Refocus with original fixture again — full triage should reappear

## Exit Criteria

All walkthrough steps pass on both fixtures. Any bugs found are fixed and notched inline before wrapping.

### deploy-and-real-data-test (₢A9AAL) [rough]

## Character

Field deployment and live validation. Ship the app to Ann's MacBook Air, then test against real Epic clipboard content. This is the proof-of-life moment — synthetic fixtures are behind us.

## Deploy

1. Build release: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh`
2. Deploy to Ann's machine: `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` (scp to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/`)
3. If app is already running on Ann's machine, the self-update watcher should detect the new bundle and relaunch automatically
4. If not running, launch manually from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`

## Real Data Test

1. On Ann's machine, open Epic and navigate to a patient chart
2. Use Epic's "Copy All" to copy a clinical note to clipboard
3. Switch to PHI Clipbuddy — triage view should appear
4. Review findings: confirm real patient name, DOB, MRN, provider names detected as RED
5. Review YELLOW findings: check for false positives on common medical terms — these should NOT appear as yellow (whitelist should filter them)
6. Toggle any legitimate non-PHI yellow items to PASS
7. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard"
8. Paste into a text editor — confirm clean output suitable for Open Evidence
9. Paste into Open Evidence and confirm it accepts the text

## What to Watch For

- **False negatives (missed PHI)**: Any patient name, DOB, MRN, or provider name that slips through — critical failure
- **False positives (over-flagging)**: Medical terms, drug names, or common English words flagged as PHI — annoying but fixable via whitelist
- **Formatting**: Is the anonymized output readable? Does it preserve enough structure for Open Evidence to understand?
- **Performance**: Any perceptible lag on focus switch or copy?
- **Self-update**: Did the watcher pick up the new bundle correctly?

## Exit Criteria

At least one real Epic note processed end-to-end: clipboard → triage → toggle → anonymize → paste into Open Evidence. No PHI leaks in anonymized output. Any issues found are documented as itches or new paces.

### ssa-download-resolution (₢A9AAM) [rough]

## Character

Interactive design decision. The premise is clear and the options are enumerated — this pace is a conversation to pick a path, then a small implementation.

## Problem

The `apcad` dictionary refresh binary cannot download SSA Baby Names data programmatically. SSA uses Akamai bot protection that 403s non-browser HTTP clients (ureq, curl). The current workaround requires a human to download `names.zip` in a browser and place it at a staging path (`.ssa_names_staging.zip`). This builds a human into a loop that should be automatable.

## Options Identified

1. **Commit the staging zip** (~7.7MB, public domain). Tool already prefers staging file over download. Eliminates human-in-loop entirely. Update by committing a new zip whenever new year data matters.
2. **Use an alternative mirror** (GitHub, Kaggle, data.gov). Avoids Akamai but trades URL stability.
3. **Browser-mimicking HTTP** (curl-impersonate, crafted TLS). Arms race with Akamai.
4. **Headless browser** (chromiumoxide, playwright). Heavy dependency for one file.
5. **Accept the status quo** — staging zip is a one-time manual step that persists indefinitely. SSA data is historical; new year files are annual.

## Deliverables

- Interactive decision on which option to pursue
- Implement the chosen resolution
- Remove or update the staging fallback messaging if no longer needed

### entity-propagation-tier (₢A9AAN) [rough]

## Character

Focused detection enhancement. Mechanical implementation following established Tier 2/3 patterns — no design ambiguity. The aho-corasick infrastructure already exists for Tier 3; this reuses it with a dynamic phrase dictionary built per-document.

## Problem

Tier 2 (label-anchored) identifies entities like "Maine Medical Center" from the `Facility:` field, but when the same phrase appears in narrative text, no tier catches it. Tier 3 (dictionary) operates on individual words, and common words like "Medical" and "Center" are whitelisted. The identifying unit is the multi-word phrase, not the individual tokens.

## Approach

Add entity propagation between Tier 2 and Tier 3 in `apcre_engine.rs`:

1. After `apcrm_scan_anchored` completes, collect the full multi-word values from labeled field spans (Facility, Patient, Attending, Provider, Referring, etc.)
2. Build a case-insensitive aho-corasick automaton from those phrases
3. Scan `plain_text` for matches — emit RED findings with a new `Propagated` mechanism variant
4. Existing merge logic (`zapcre_merge_findings`) handles overlap with Tier 2's original findings

**Whitespace normalization**: Already handled by the parser (`zapcrp_normalize_whitespace`) — both `plain_text` and span values are single-space-normalized. No additional normalization needed.

**Category**: Inherit the category from the originating Tier 2 finding (Facility stays Facility, Provider stays Provider, etc.)

## Scope

- Modify `apcrm_match.rs`: add `Propagated` to `apcrm_Mechanism` enum
- Modify `apcre_engine.rs`: add propagation scan between Tier 2 and Tier 3 calls
- Add unit tests for propagation (facility in narrative, provider in signature block)
- No frontend changes needed — findings render the same regardless of mechanism

The current workaround requires a human to download `names.zip` in a browser and place it at a staging path (`.ssa_names_staging.zip`). This builds a human into a loop that should be automatable.

## Options Identified

1. **Commit the staging zip** (~7.7MB, public domain). Tool already prefers staging file over download. Eliminates human-in-loop entirely. Update by committing a new zip whenever new year data matters.
2. **Use an alternative mirror** (GitHub, Kaggle, data.gov). Avoids Akamai but trades URL stability.
3. **Browser-mimicking HTTP** (curl-impersonate, crafted TLS). Arms race with Akamai.
4. **Headless browser** (chromiumoxide, playwright). Heavy dependency for one file.
5. **Accept the status quo** — staging zip is a one-time manual step that persists indefinitely. SSA data is historical; new year files are annual.

## Deliverables

- Interactive decision on which option to pursue
- Implement the chosen resolution
- Remove or update the staging fallback messaging if no longer needed

### entity-propagation-tier (₢A9AAN) [rough]

## Character

Focused detection enhancement. Mechanical implementation following established Tier 2/3 patterns — no design ambiguity. The aho-corasick infrastructure already exists for Tier 3; this reuses it with a dynamic phrase dictionary built per-document.

## Problem

Tier 2 (label-anchored) identifies entities like "Maine Medical Center" from the `Facility:` field, but when the same phrase appears in narrative text, no tier catches it. Tier 3 (dictionary) operates on individual words, and common words like "Medical" and "Center" are whitelisted. The identifying unit is the multi-word phrase, not the individual tokens.

## Approach

Add entity propagation between Tier 2 and Tier 3 in `apcre_engine.rs`:

1. After `apcrm_scan_anchored` completes, collect the full multi-word values from labeled field spans (Facility, Patient, Attending, Provider, Referring, etc.)
2. Build a case-insensitive aho-corasick automaton from those phrases
3. Scan `plain_text` for matches — emit RED findings with a new `Propagated` mechanism variant
4. Existing merge logic (`zapcre_merge_findings`) handles overlap with Tier 2's original findings

**Whitespace normalization**: Already handled by the parser (`zapcrp_normalize_whitespace`) — both `plain_text` and span values are single-space-normalized. No additional normalization needed.

**Category**: Inherit the category from the originating Tier 2 finding (Facility stays Facility, Provider stays Provider, etc.)

## Scope

- Modify `apcrm_match.rs`: add `Propagated` to `apcrm_Mechanism` enum
- Modify `apcre_engine.rs`: add propagation scan between Tier 2 and Tier 3 calls
- Add unit tests for propagation (facility in narrative, provider in signature block)
- No frontend changes needed — findings render the same regardless of mechanism

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣A9
2	
3	## Character
4	
5	Product design into prototype implementation. Starts with synthesis (specs capturing a rich design conversation), transitions to exploratory engineering (Tauri + Rust + cross-platform clipboard), then steady implementation of a three-tier PHI detection engine. Requires careful attention to minting discipline — this kit lives alongside RBK/JJK/BUK and must respect the prefix universe.
6	
7	## Problem
8	
9	Clinicians using Epic EHR need to consult Open Evidence (an AI diagnostic tool) but must not paste Protected Health Information (PHI) into it. HIPAA Safe Harbor defines 18 PHI categories that must be removed. Today, clinicians either skip the consultation or manually scrub text — both are unacceptable. No clipboard-level tool exists to automate the triage.
10	
11	## Solution
12	
13	Ann's PHI Clipbuddy (`apcap`) — a Tauri desktop app (macOS, later Windows) that:
14	1. Validates clipboard content as clinical via heuristic, clears system clipboard after consumption
15	2. Parses Epic "Copy All" HTML for structural context (labels, sections)
16	3. Runs a three-tier PHI detection engine (regex → label-anchored → dictionary)
17	4. Displays a triage view: RED (definite PHI), YELLOW (questionable), GREY (pass)
18	5. Lets clinician toggle findings — YELLOW easily, RED with deliberate friction (separate panel section)
19	6. Writes anonymized plain text to clipboard with category-specific placeholders (DOB→age, >89→"90+")
20	7. Self-updates from `/Users/Shared/apcua/` without confirmation
21	
22	## Key Design Decisions
23	
24	- **Anchored vs Matched**: Tier 2 (label position) and Tier 3 (dictionary identity) are independent mechanisms with distinct vocabulary — see APCPS
25	- **Case-insensitive**: All dictionary lookups normalize to lowercase; original case preserved in display
26	- **Per-word anchoring**: No multi-word merge — each word individually classified
27	- **Conservative bias**: False positive preferred over false negative
28	- **Provider/facility names**: Treated as PHI (re-identifying in combination with clinical details)
29	- **Clipboard always cleared** after consumption
30	- **Clinical content heuristic** gates processing; non-clinical clipboard shows diagnostic and clears triage
31	
32	## Authoritative Documents
33	
34	| Document | Owns | Path |
35	|----------|------|------|
36	| Product Spec (APCS) | UX plan, workflow, deployment, HIPAA context | `Tools/apck/APCS-Specification.md` |
37	| Prototype Spec (APCPS) | Detection pipeline, tech stack, dictionaries, project structure, scope boundaries | `Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` |
38	| Claude Context | Build/run/deploy instructions, file mappings, prefix tree, tabtargets | `Tools/apck/apck-claude-context.md` |
39	| Rust Coding Guide (RCG) | Naming conventions, test patterns, disciplines | `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md` |

48	
49	- App builds clean: `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` or `cargo build` in apcd/
50	- Two fixture loaders available: `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` and `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh`
51	
52	## Walkthrough — Progress Note Fixture
53	
54	1. Run `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` to launch the app
55	2. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.progress.sh` to load progress note HTML onto clipboard
56	3. Switch to the app window — triage view should appear
57	4. Confirm: RED findings include Patient (Margaret Thornton), Attending (Dr. Sarah Whitfield), DOB date, SSN, MRN, phone, email, address, URL
58	5. Confirm: YELLOW findings include dictionary-matched names in narrative
59	6. Confirm: DOB shows the date highlighted, not an age yet (age appears in anonymized output)
60	7. Toggle two YELLOW items to PASS — confirm preview updates (grey styling)
61	8. Toggle one RED item to PASS — confirm preview updates
62	9. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" — button should show "Copied!"
63	10. Paste into a text editor. Verify:
64	    - Elided items show category placeholders: `[NAME]`, `[SSN]`, `[PHONE]`, `[EMAIL]`, `[ADDRESS]`, etc.
65	    - DOB shows `Age: 74` (or appropriate computed age)
66	    - Items toggled to PASS show original text
67	    - Line breaks and section structure preserved — readable, not a wall
68	    - No PHI leaks in elided regions
69	
70	## Walkthrough — Geriatric Consult Fixture
71	
72	1. Run `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.geriatric.sh` to load geriatric HTML onto clipboard
73	2. Switch to app — triage view should refresh with new content
74	3. Confirm: clinical content detected, findings populated
75	4. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard" with all defaults (everything elided)
76	5. Paste and verify: all PHI replaced, structure preserved
77	6. If geriatric fixture has a DOB for patient >89 years old, confirm `Age: 90+` appears
78	
79	## Edge Cases to Probe
80	
81	- Refocus without changing clipboard — should show "unchanged", triage preserved
82	- Copy non-clinical text (e.g., "hello world") — should show non-clinical diagnostic, triage cleared
83	- After non-clinical, click "Copy Anonymized" — should show error (no clinical data)
84	- Refocus with original fixture again — full triage should reappear
85	
86	## Exit Criteria
87	
88	All walkthrough steps pass on both fixtures. Any bugs found are fixed and notched inline before wrapping.

100	3. If app is already running on Ann's machine, the self-update watcher should detect the new bundle and relaunch automatically
101	4. If not running, launch manually from `/Users/Shared/apcua/`

110	6. Toggle any legitimate non-PHI yellow items to PASS
111	7. Click "Copy Anonymized to Clipboard"
112	8. Paste into a text editor — confirm clean output suitable for Open Evidence

118	- **False positives (over-flagging)**: Medical terms, drug names, or common English words flagged as PHI — annoying but fixable via whitelist

120	- **Performance**: Any perceptible lag on focus switch or copy?

122	
123	## Exit Criteria
124	
125	At least one real Epic note processed end-to-end: clipboard → triage → toggle → anonymize → paste into Open Evidence. No PHI leaks in anonymized output. Any issues found are documented as itches or new paces.

182	
183	## Character
184	
185	Focused build-out of a new binary and its tabtarget integration. Mechanical Rust + BUK wiring — the detection engine and parser already exist as library functions. No design ambiguity; the format decisions are made.
186	
187	## Deliverables
188	
189	New binary `apcab` (App Batch): reads `*.html` files from an input directory, runs the parse and detection pipeline (apcrp + apcre), writes one `{stem}.assay.txt` per input file to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.
190	
191	Assay output format — guillemet-delimited elision markers with PHI flowing through:
192	```
193	Patient: ‹RED:PATIENT Margaret Thornton›
194	DOB: ‹RED:DOB 03/15/1952›
195	...presented to ‹YELLOW:FACILITY Maine Medical Center› with...
196	```
197	Unelided text passes through verbatim. Unamended classification — no toggle logic, pure engine output.
198	
199	## Implementation
200	
201	- `Tools/apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` — new binary entry point
202	  - Accept input directory as CLI argument
203	  - Glob `*.html`, parse each, run detection engine, render assay text
204	  - Write `{stem}.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`
205	  - No Tauri dependency — library crates only (apcrp, apcre, apcrm, apcrd)
206	- Register `apcab` in `Cargo.toml` as a `[[bin]]` entry
207	- Tabtarget `tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh` — folio via param1 (input directory path)
208	- Enroll colophon `apcw-ba` in zipper, wire through workbench
209	- Update `apck-claude-context.md` prefix tree and file mappings
210	
211	## Default invocation
212	
213	```
214	tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh Tools/apck/test_fixtures/
215	```
216	
217	Emits `epic_progress_note.assay.txt` and `epic_geriatric_consult.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.
218	
219	## Exit Criteria
220	
221	Both fixture HTML files produce assay output. Elision markers present for known PHI in each fixture. Output is readable as a document with classifications visible inline.

## Problem

The `apcad` dictionary refresh binary cannot download SSA Baby Names data programmatically. SSA uses Akamai bot protection that 403s non-browser HTTP clients (ureq, curl). The current workaround requires a human to download `names.zip` in a browser and place it at a staging path (`.ssa_names_staging.zip`). This builds a human into a loop that should be automatable.

## Options Identified

1. **Commit the staging zip** (~7.7MB, public domain). Tool already prefers staging file over download. Eliminates human-in-loop entirely. Update by committing a new zip whenever new year data matters.
2. **Use an alternative mirror** (GitHub, Kaggle, data.gov). Avoids Akamai but trades URL stability.
3. **Browser-mimicking HTTP** (curl-impersonate, crafted TLS). Arms race with Akamai.
4. **Headless browser** (chromiumoxide, playwright). Heavy dependency for one file.
5. **Accept the status quo** — staging zip is a one-time manual step that persists indefinitely. SSA data is historical; new year files are annual.

## Deliverables

- Interactive decision on which option to pursue
- Implement the chosen resolution
- Remove or update the staging fallback messaging if no longer needed

# jjezs_pace ₢A9AAN

## Character

Focused detection enhancement. Mechanical implementation following established Tier 2/3 patterns — no design ambiguity. The aho-corasick infrastructure already exists for Tier 3; this reuses it with a dynamic phrase dictionary built per-document.

## Problem

Tier 2 (label-anchored) identifies entities like "Maine Medical Center" from the `Facility:` field, but when the same phrase appears in narrative text, no tier catches it. Tier 3 (dictionary) operates on individual words, and common words like "Medical" and "Center" are whitelisted. The identifying unit is the multi-word phrase, not the individual tokens.

## Approach

Add entity propagation between Tier 2 and Tier 3 in `apcre_engine.rs`:

1. After `apcrm_scan_anchored` completes, collect the full multi-word values from labeled field spans (Facility, Patient, Attending, Provider, Referring, etc.)
2. Build a case-insensitive aho-corasick automaton from those phrases
3. Scan `plain_text` for matches — emit RED findings with a new `Propagated` mechanism variant
4. Existing merge logic (`zapcre_merge_findings`) handles overlap with Tier 2's original findings

**Whitespace normalization**: Already handled by the parser (`zapcrp_normalize_whitespace`) — both `plain_text` and span values are single-space-normalized. No additional normalization needed.

**Category**: Inherit the category from the originating Tier 2 finding (Facility stays Facility, Provider stays Provider, etc.)

## Scope

- Modify `apcrm_match.rs`: add `Propagated` to `apcrm_Mechanism` enum
- Modify `apcre_engine.rs`: add propagation scan between Tier 2 and Tier 3 calls
- Add unit tests for propagation (facility in narrative, provider in signature block)
- No frontend changes needed — findings render the same regardless of mechanism

# jjezs_pace ₢A9AAO

## Character

Focused build-out of a new binary and its tabtarget integration. Mechanical Rust + BUK wiring — the detection engine and parser already exist as library functions. No design ambiguity; the format decisions are made.

## Deliverables

New binary `apcab` (App Batch): reads `*.html` files from an input directory, runs the parse and detection pipeline (apcrp + apcre), writes one `{stem}.assay.txt` per input file to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.

Assay output format — guillemet-delimited elision markers with PHI flowing through:
```
Patient: ‹RED:PATIENT Margaret Thornton›
DOB: ‹RED:DOB 03/15/1952›
...presented to ‹YELLOW:FACILITY Maine Medical Center› with...
```
Unelided text passes through verbatim. Unamended classification — no toggle logic, pure engine output.

## Implementation

- `Tools/apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` — new binary entry point
  - Accept input directory as CLI argument
  - Glob `*.html`, parse each, run detection engine, render assay text
  - Write `{stem}.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`
  - No Tauri dependency — library crates only (apcrp, apcre, apcrm, apcrd)
- Register `apcab` in `Cargo.toml` as a `[[bin]]` entry
- Tabtarget `tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh` — folio via param1 (input directory path)
- Enroll colophon `apcw-ba` in zipper, wire through workbench
- Update `apck-claude-context.md` prefix tree and file mappings

## Default invocation

```
tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh Tools/apck/test_fixtures/
```

Emits `epic_progress_note.assay.txt` and `epic_geriatric_consult.assay.txt` to `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`.

## Exit Criteria

Both fixture HTML files produce assay output. Elision markers present for known PHI in each fixture. Output is readable as a document with classifications visible inline.
