// end::mapping-section[]


== Overview

This document defines the formal vocabulary for the detection pipeline in Ann's PHI Clipbuddy.
It specifies the concrete entities the algorithm operates on, the resources it consults, and the steps it performs.

The application specification (APCAS) owns the user experience.
The prototype specification (APCPS) owns implementation details, tech stack, and data sources.
This document owns the detection algorithm's conceptual structure — the terms that must be precise for the pipeline to be reasoned about correctly.


== Input

[[apcs_clipboard_content]]
{apcs_clipboard_content}::
Raw content read from the system clipboard on window focus.
May be HTML or plain text.
Consumed once, then the system clipboard is cleared.

[[apcs_epic_html]]
{apcs_epic_html}::
HTML produced by Epic's "Copy All" function.
Semi-structured: bold labels for demographics, section headers for clinical categories, narrative freetext for clinical prose, signature blocks for provider attribution.
The structural formatting is the primary advantage over plain text — labels anchor PHI detection with high precision.

[[apcs_clinical_gate]]
{apcs_clinical_gate}::
A heuristic that decides whether
{apcs_clipboard_content}
is clinical.
Scans for known label patterns (Patient:, DOB:, MRN:, etc.).
Acceptance threshold: two or more recognized labels present.
Content that fails the
{apcs_clinical_gate}
is not processed; the triage view clears.


== Parsed Structure

[[apcs_document]]
{apcs_document}::
The result of parsing
{apcs_epic_html}.
Contains a sequence of typed
{apcs_span_s}
and the derived
{apcs_plain_text}.

[[apcs_plain_text]]
{apcs_plain_text}::
A single string derived from the
{apcs_document}
by stripping HTML markup, normalizing whitespace, and concatenating span contributions with structural line breaks.
All detection scans operate against
{apcs_plain_text}.
Offsets in
{apcs_finding_s}
are byte positions within this string.

[[apcs_span]]
{apcs_span}::
A typed region within the
{apcs_document}.
Each
{apcs_span}
has a kind, an optional label, text content, and a byte offset into
{apcs_plain_text}.
Three kinds:
{apcs_labeled_field},
{apcs_section_header},
{apcs_narrative}.

[[apcs_labeled_field]]
{apcs_labeled_field}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label followed by a value (e.g., `Patient: Margaret J. Thornton`).
The label is the bold text before the colon.
The value is the text after the colon, extending to the next structural boundary (another label, line break, or HTML element boundary).

[[apcs_section_header]]
{apcs_section_header}::
A
{apcs_span}
produced by a bold label matching a known clinical section name (Chief Complaint, History of Present Illness, Assessment/Plan, etc.).
Contributes the header text to
{apcs_plain_text}
but carries no value — the section body follows as subsequent
{apcs_span_s}.

[[apcs_narrative]]
{apcs_narrative}::
A
{apcs_span}
containing freetext not attached to a label.
Clinical prose, family history details, plan items.
PHI in
{apcs_narrative}
{apcs_span_s}
lacks positional anchoring and must be detected by the
{apcs_regex_scan}
or
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.


== Detection Resources

=== Registries

[[apcs_label_registry]]
{apcs_label_registry}::
The enumerated set of
{apcs_labeled_field}
labels that trigger per-word PHI extraction in the
{apcs_label_scan}.
Each entry specifies the label text (case-insensitive match) and the
{apcs_phi_category}
assigned to extracted words.
+
[cols="2,1"]
|===
| Label | Category

| Patient:           | NAME
| Attending:         | PROVIDER
| Provider:          | PROVIDER
| Referring:         | PROVIDER
| Facility:          | FACILITY
| Electronically signed by: | PROVIDER
| Emergency Contact  | NAME
| Primary Care Physician | PROVIDER
|===

[[apcs_regex_table]]
{apcs_regex_table}::
The enumerated set of regular expression patterns that detect structurally identifiable PHI in the
{apcs_regex_scan}.
Each entry specifies a pattern, a
{apcs_phi_category},
and a capture group index.
All matches produce
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding_s}.
See APCPS for the full pattern table.

=== Dictionaries

[[apcs_blacklist]]
{apcs_blacklist}::
A word list whose members are flagged as potential PHI during the
{apcs_dictionary_scan}.
Three named
{apcs_blacklist_s}
are combined into a single lookup automaton:

[[apcs_surname_dictionary]]
{apcs_surname_dictionary}::
US Census 2010 surnames, top 1,000 by frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_firstname_dictionary]]
{apcs_firstname_dictionary}::
SSA baby names, top 1,000 by aggregate frequency.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_city_dictionary]]
{apcs_city_dictionary}::
US state capitals plus top 100 cities by population.
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_whitelist]]
{apcs_whitelist}::
A word list whose members are common English or medical terms.
Membership in a
{apcs_whitelist}
does not prevent a
{apcs_finding} —
a word in both a
{apcs_blacklist}
and a
{apcs_whitelist}
is classified
{apcs_yellow}.
Two named
{apcs_whitelist_s}:

[[apcs_english_whitelist]]
{apcs_english_whitelist}::
Top 5,000 common English words (Norvig/Google n-grams).
Source: public domain.

[[apcs_medical_whitelist]]
{apcs_medical_whitelist}::
Hand-curated medications, lab tests, diagnoses, anatomy, abbreviations.
Source: original curation.


== Finding Anatomy

[[apcs_finding]]
{apcs_finding}::
A classified region of
{apcs_plain_text}.
Produced by one of the three detection scans.
Each
{apcs_finding}
carries: the matched text, a byte offset and length in
{apcs_plain_text},
a
{apcs_severity},
a
{apcs_phi_category},
and a replacement string for anonymization.

[[apcs_severity]]
{apcs_severity}::
The confidence level of a
{apcs_finding}.
Determines default elision behavior and visual presentation.
Three levels:

[[apcs_red]]
{apcs_red}::
Definite PHI.
Produced by the
{apcs_regex_scan}
(structural match) or the
{apcs_label_scan}
(positional authority).
Default: elide.

[[apcs_yellow]]
{apcs_yellow}::
Possible PHI.
Produced by the
{apcs_dictionary_scan}
when a word appears in a
{apcs_blacklist}.
Default: elide.

[[apcs_pass]]
{apcs_pass}::
Not PHI.
A word appearing only in a
{apcs_whitelist}
and not in any
{apcs_blacklist}.
No
{apcs_finding}
is emitted for
{apcs_pass}
words.

[[apcs_phi_category]]
{apcs_phi_category}::
The HIPAA Safe Harbor category of a
{apcs_finding}.
Determines the
{apcs_placeholder}
text used in
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Enumerated: NAME, PROVIDER, FACILITY, DATE, DOB, PHONE, EMAIL, SSN, MRN, ACCOUNT, ENCOUNTER_ID, ADDRESS, HEALTH_PLAN_ID, DEVICE_ID, NPI, DEA, URL, IP_ADDRESS.


== Pipeline Steps

The detection pipeline executes five steps in fixed order.
Each step either transforms input or produces
{apcs_finding_s}.

[[apcs_parse]]
{apcs_parse}::
Transform
{apcs_epic_html}
into a
{apcs_document}:
a sequence of typed
{apcs_span_s}
and derived
{apcs_plain_text}.
HTML structure is consumed; output is position-mapped text.

[[apcs_regex_scan]]
{apcs_regex_scan}::
Scan
{apcs_plain_text}
against every pattern in the
{apcs_regex_table}.
Each match emits a
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding}
with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the pattern.

[[apcs_label_scan]]
{apcs_label_scan}::
For each
{apcs_labeled_field}
whose label appears in the
{apcs_label_registry}:
extract the value text, segment into words, and emit one
{apcs_red}
{apcs_finding}
per word with the
{apcs_phi_category}
specified by the registry entry.

[[apcs_dictionary_scan]]
{apcs_dictionary_scan}::
Scan
{apcs_plain_text}
against the combined
{apcs_blacklist}
automaton.
For each hit, check
{apcs_whitelist}
membership.
Emit a
{apcs_yellow}
{apcs_finding}
if the word is in any
{apcs_blacklist}
(regardless of
{apcs_whitelist}
membership).
Words in a
{apcs_whitelist}
only receive no
{apcs_finding}.

[[apcs_merge]]
{apcs_merge}::
Combine all
{apcs_finding_s}
from the three scans.
When multiple
{apcs_finding_s}
occupy the same byte range, retain only the one with highest
{apcs_severity}
({apcs_red} > {apcs_yellow} > {apcs_pass}).


== Output Projections

[[apcs_anonymized_text]]
{apcs_anonymized_text}::
{apcs_plain_text}
with elided
{apcs_finding_s}
replaced by their
{apcs_placeholder_s}.
Findings toggled to
{apcs_pass}
by the clinician pass through with original text.
The output format for pasting into Open Evidence.

[[apcs_placeholder]]
{apcs_placeholder}::
A category-specific substitution string used in
{apcs_anonymized_text}.
Format: `[CATEGORY]` (e.g., `[NAME]`, `[PHONE]`, `[ADDRESS]`).
Exception: DOB emits `Age: NN` (computed from date of birth and current date; ages over 89 emit `Age: 90+` per HIPAA Safe Harbor).

[[apcs_assay]]
{apcs_assay}::
{apcs_plain_text}
with
{apcs_finding_s}
annotated inline using
{apcs_elision_marker_s}.
A diagnostic projection — shows the detection decisions within the document for review.
Not user-facing; used for pipeline quality assessment.

[[apcs_elision_marker]]
{apcs_elision_marker}::
The annotation unit in an
{apcs_assay}.
Format: `‹SEVERITY:CATEGORY matched text›` (guillemet-delimited).
Shows the
{apcs_severity},
{apcs_phi_category},
and original text of each
{apcs_finding}
inline with surrounding
{apcs_plain_text}.

## Ann's PHI Clipbuddy Kit (APCK)

APCK is a Tauri desktop app that intercepts clipboard content from Epic EHR, detects PHI via a three-tier engine (regex, label-anchored, dictionary), presents a red/yellow/grey triage view, and writes anonymized plain text to clipboard for pasting into Open Evidence.

### File Acronym Mappings

| Acronym | File | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| **APCAS** | `apck/APCAS-Specification.md` | Application spec — UX, workflow, deployment |
| **APCPS** | `apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` | Prototype spec — detection pipeline, tech stack |
| **APCW** | `apck/apcw_workbench.sh` | Workbench |
| **APCZ** | `apck/apcz_zipper.sh` | Zipper enrollment |
| **APCC** | `apck/apcc_cli.sh` | CLI command implementations |
| **APCAP** | `apck/apcd/src/apcap_main.rs` | Tauri app entry point |
| **APCAL** | `apck/apcd/src/apcal_main.rs` | Fixture loader (clipboard writer) |
| **APCAD** | `apck/apcd/src/apcad_main.rs` | Dictionary refresh (downloads public sources, regenerates dictionaries) |
| **APCAB** | `apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` | Batch assay (run detection pipeline on HTML directory, write assay output) |

**Source modules:**

| Source | Prefix | Test | Prefix | Purpose |
|--------|--------|------|--------|---------|
| `apcrl_log.rs` | `apcrl` | — | — | Logging macros (`apcrl_info!`, `apcrl_error!`, `apcrl_fatal!`) |
| `apcre_engine.rs` | `apcre` | `apcte_engine.rs` | `apcte` | PHI detection orchestrator |
| `apcrp_parse.rs` | `apcrp` | `apctp_parse.rs` | `apctp` | HTML clipboard parsing |
| `apcrm_match.rs` | `apcrm` | `apctm_match.rs` | `apctm` | Dictionary/regex matching |
| `apcrd_dictionaries.rs` | `apcrd` | `apctd_dictionaries.rs` | `apctd` | Dictionary loading |
| `apcru_update.rs` | `apcru` | — | — | Self-update watcher (no unit tests — I/O + process) |

**Other key paths:**
- `Tools/apck/apcd/ui/` — Frontend (HTML/CSS only — no JavaScript)
- `Tools/apck/apcd/dictionaries/` — Blacklist/whitelist data files
- `Tools/apck/test_fixtures/` — Synthetic Epic clipboard data

**No JavaScript rule:** All rendering logic lives in Rust. The Tauri webview is a passive display surface — Rust pushes complete HTML on every state change via `eval()`. Inline `onclick` attributes use the Tauri bridge primitive (`window.__TAURI__.core.invoke`) for command dispatch. No `.js` files, no JS application logic. This eliminates the Rust-JS language boundary as a bug class.

**Rust conventions:** Follow RCG (`Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md`). Source prefix: `apcr{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Test prefix: `apct{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Classifier matches between source and test.

### Tabtargets

| Tabtarget | Colophon | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` | `apcw-b` | `cargo tauri build` (release) |
| `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` | `apcw-r` | `cargo run --bin apcap` (local development) |
| `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` | `apcw-d` | Build + scp to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/` |
| `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` | `apcw-fl` | Run `apcal` to load fixture HTML onto clipboard |
| `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` | `apcw-t` | `cargo test` in `apcd/` |
| `tt/apcw-dr.DictionaryRefresh.sh` | `apcw-dr` | `cargo run --bin apcad` (refresh dictionaries from public sources) |
| `tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh` | `apcw-ba` | `cargo run --bin apcab` (batch assay on HTML directory) |

### Prefix Tree

```
apc  (non-terminal)
├── apca   (non-terminal)
│   ├── apcab  — App Batch binary (assay — detection pipeline on HTML files)
│   ├── apcad  — App Dictionary binary (refresh from public sources)
│   ├── apcal  — App Loader binary (fixture clipboard tool)
│   └── apcap  — App Prototype binary (Tauri main)
├── apcc   — CLI command implementations
├── apcd   — Rust/Tauri source directory
│   └── apcrl  — Logging macros (info, error, fatal with file/line)
├── apck   — kit directory
├── apcps  — prototype specification document
├── apcs   — product specification document
├── apcu   (non-terminal)
│   └── apcua — update staging directory (/Users/Shared/apcua/)
├── apcw   — workbench
└── apcz   — zipper
```

### Detection Architecture

Three tiers, merged by highest severity:
- **Tier 1 — Regex (RED):** SSN, phone, email, dates, addresses, zip, labeled identifiers
- **Tier 2 — Label-anchored (RED):** Words following Epic labels (Patient:, Attending:, Facility:, etc.)
- **Tier 3 — Dictionary (YELLOW/PASS):** aho-corasick blacklist scan, whitelist filter, collision → YELLOW

### Deploy Workflow

1. `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` — produces `.app` bundle
2. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` — scp to Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`
3. App self-updates: watches staging directory, copies new bundle over self, relaunches

lines: -| **APCPS** | `apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` | Prototype spec — detection pipeline, tech stack |
+| **APCS0** | `apck/APCS0-SpecTop.adoc` | Detection pipeline spec — formal vocabulary (MCM concept model) |
+| **APCPS** | `apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` | Prototype spec — tech stack, data sources, project structure |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
## Ann's PHI Clipbuddy Kit (APCK)

APCK is a Tauri desktop app that intercepts clipboard content from Epic EHR, detects PHI via a three-tier engine (regex, label-anchored, dictionary), presents a red/yellow/grey triage view, and writes anonymized plain text to clipboard for pasting into Open Evidence.

### File Acronym Mappings

| Acronym | File | Description |
|---------|------|-------------|
| **APCAS** | `apck/APCAS-Specification.md` | Application spec — UX, workflow, deployment |
| **APCS0** | `apck/APCS0-SpecTop.adoc` | Detection pipeline spec — formal vocabulary (MCM concept model) |
| **APCPS** | `apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md` | Prototype spec — tech stack, data sources, project structure |
| **APCW** | `apck/apcw_workbench.sh` | Workbench |
| **APCZ** | `apck/apcz_zipper.sh` | Zipper enrollment |
| **APCC** | `apck/apcc_cli.sh` | CLI command implementations |
| **APCAP** | `apck/apcd/src/apcap_main.rs` | Tauri app entry point |
| **APCAL** | `apck/apcd/src/apcal_main.rs` | Fixture loader (clipboard writer) |
| **APCAD** | `apck/apcd/src/apcad_main.rs` | Dictionary refresh (downloads public sources, regenerates dictionaries) |
| **APCAB** | `apck/apcd/src/apcab_main.rs` | Batch assay (run detection pipeline on HTML directory, write assay output) |

**Source modules:**

| Source | Prefix | Test | Prefix | Purpose |
|--------|--------|------|--------|---------|
| `apcrl_log.rs` | `apcrl` | — | — | Logging macros (`apcrl_info!`, `apcrl_error!`, `apcrl_fatal!`) |
| `apcre_engine.rs` | `apcre` | `apcte_engine.rs` | `apcte` | PHI detection orchestrator |
| `apcrp_parse.rs` | `apcrp` | `apctp_parse.rs` | `apctp` | HTML clipboard parsing |
| `apcrm_match.rs` | `apcrm` | `apctm_match.rs` | `apctm` | Dictionary/regex matching |
| `apcrd_dictionaries.rs` | `apcrd` | `apctd_dictionaries.rs` | `apctd` | Dictionary loading |
| `apcru_update.rs` | `apcru` | — | — | Self-update watcher (no unit tests — I/O + process) |

**Other key paths:**
- `Tools/apck/apcd/ui/` — Frontend (HTML/CSS only — no JavaScript)
- `Tools/apck/apcd/dictionaries/` — Blacklist/whitelist data files
- `Tools/apck/test_fixtures/` — Synthetic Epic clipboard data

**No JavaScript rule:** All rendering logic lives in Rust. The Tauri webview is a passive display surface — Rust pushes complete HTML on every state change via `eval()`. Inline `onclick` attributes use the Tauri bridge primitive (`window.__TAURI__.core.invoke`) for command dispatch. No `.js` files, no JS application logic. This eliminates the Rust-JS language boundary as a bug class.

**Rust conventions:** Follow RCG (`Tools/vok/vov_veiled/RCG-RustCodingGuide.md`). Source prefix: `apcr{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Test prefix: `apct{classifier}_{name}.rs`. Classifier matches between source and test.

### Tabtargets

| Tabtarget | Colophon | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` | `apcw-b` | `cargo tauri build` (release) |
| `tt/apcw-r.Run.sh` | `apcw-r` | `cargo run --bin apcap` (local development) |
| `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` | `apcw-d` | Build + scp to `anns-macbook-air:/Users/Shared/apcua/` |
| `tt/apcw-fl.FixtureLoad.sh` | `apcw-fl` | Run `apcal` to load fixture HTML onto clipboard |
| `tt/apcw-t.Test.sh` | `apcw-t` | `cargo test` in `apcd/` |
| `tt/apcw-dr.DictionaryRefresh.sh` | `apcw-dr` | `cargo run --bin apcad` (refresh dictionaries from public sources) |
| `tt/apcw-ba.BatchAssay.sh` | `apcw-ba` | `cargo run --bin apcab` (batch assay on HTML directory) |

### Prefix Tree

```
apc  (non-terminal)
├── apca   (non-terminal)
│   ├── apcab  — App Batch binary (assay — detection pipeline on HTML files)
│   ├── apcad  — App Dictionary binary (refresh from public sources)
│   ├── apcal  — App Loader binary (fixture clipboard tool)
│   └── apcap  — App Prototype binary (Tauri main)
├── apcc   — CLI command implementations
├── apcd   — Rust/Tauri source directory
│   └── apcrl  — Logging macros (info, error, fatal with file/line)
├── apck   — kit directory
├── apcps  — prototype specification document
├── apcs   — product specification document
├── apcu   (non-terminal)
│   └── apcua — update staging directory (/Users/Shared/apcua/)
├── apcw   — workbench
└── apcz   — zipper
```

### Detection Architecture

Three tiers, merged by highest severity:
- **Tier 1 — Regex (RED):** SSN, phone, email, dates, addresses, zip, labeled identifiers
- **Tier 2 — Label-anchored (RED):** Words following Epic labels (Patient:, Attending:, Facility:, etc.)
- **Tier 3 — Dictionary (YELLOW/PASS):** aho-corasick blacklist scan, whitelist filter, collision → YELLOW

### Deploy Workflow

1. `tt/apcw-b.Build.sh` — produces `.app` bundle
2. `tt/apcw-d.Deploy.sh` — scp to Ann's machine at `/Users/Shared/apcua/`
3. App self-updates: watches staging directory, copies new bundle over self, relaunches

All clean. Here's what was done:

**New document**: `APCS0-SpecTop.adoc` — MCM concept model with `apcs_` prefix quoins covering:

- **Input**: clipboard content, Epic HTML, clinical gate
- **Parsed structure**: document, plain text, span (labeled field, section header, narrative)
- **Detection resources**: label registry (enumerated table), regex table, three named blacklists (surname, firstname, city), two named whitelists (english, medical)
- **Finding anatomy**: finding, severity (RED/YELLOW/PASS), PHI category (18 enumerated)
- **Pipeline steps**: parse, regex scan, label scan, dictionary scan, merge
- **Output projections**: anonymized text, placeholder, assay, elision marker

Every entity is a quoin with an anchor, mapping entry, and definition. The abstract concepts ("collision", "propagation") are gone — replaced by concrete things you can enumerate, extend, and test against.

message.content: This is extremely rough.  Help me see the difference between a dictionary and a blacklist and a whitelist.

content.text:
Right. I conflated three things:

A **dictionary** is a word list with a source and a name. Surname Dictionary, Firstname Dictionary, City Dictionary, English Whitelist, Medical Whitelist — those are five dictionaries.

A **blacklist** is not a separate thing. It's a role: the surname, firstname, and city dictionaries serve as blacklists. The english and medical dictionaries serve as whitelists. "Blacklist" and "whitelist" are how the algorithm *uses* a dictionary, not what a dictionary *is*.

So the actual nouns are just the five named dictionaries, and the algorithm step (dictionary scan) defines which ones are consulted for flagging vs. filtering.

That's cleaner — but it still doesn't feel right. The combined automaton built from the three flagging dictionaries is a thing the code constructs and the algorithm operates on. Does that thing deserve a name, or is it an implementation detail?
